1	\chapter{The tautological quotient and the universal property of $\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)$}
2	\label{chap:Picard_GrassmannianQuot}
3	
4	\providecommand{\ShMod}{\mathrm{SheafOfModules}}
5	
6	The previous chapter constructed the Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over
7	\(\mathbb{Z}\) by gluing the affine charts \(U^I\)
8	(\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) along the transition isomorphisms
9	(\cref{def:gr_transition}) satisfying the cocycle condition
10	(\cref{lem:gr_cocycle}), and proved that the structure morphism
11	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is separated (\cref{lem:gr_separated})
12	and proper (\cref{lem:gr_proper}). This chapter adds the next layer: the
13	\emph{tautological rank-\(d\) quotient}
14	\(u : \mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)}^{\,r} \twoheadrightarrow \mathcal{U}\) of the
15	trivial rank-\(r\) sheaf, and the \emph{universal property} that makes
16	\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) the fine moduli space of rank-\(d\) locally free quotients of
17	\(\mathcal{O}^{r}\) -- equivalently, the representing scheme of the Quot functor
18	of the trivial sheaf.
19	
20	Throughout, \(r \ge d \ge 1\) are the fixed integers of the previous chapter, and
21	for a size-\(d\) subset \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) we write \(R^I\) for the
22	chart ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) of \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) and \(R^I_J\) for the
23	localisation along \(P^I_J = \det(X^I_J)\) defining the overlap
24	\(U^I_J \subseteq U^I\).
25	
26	\section{Infrastructure: locally free sheaves and module gluing}
27	\label{sec:grquot_infra}
28	
29	The constructions of this chapter -- the universal quotient sheaf
30	\(\mathcal{U}\), the tautological quotient \(u\), and the functor of points -- rest
31	on two pieces of foundational machinery for sheaves of modules. The first is the
32	predicate ``locally free of rank \(d\)'' (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}),
33	which records that a sheaf of modules is, on the members of some open cover,
34	isomorphic to the trivial bundle \(\mathcal{O}^{d}\); it is the condition cut out
35	in the definition of the Grassmannian functor. That predicate is the project-local
36	\(\mathrm{SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank}\) already set up in the Quot-scheme
37	chapter (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}), which this chapter reuses verbatim;
38	the only foundational machinery genuinely new to this chapter is the second piece:
39	a gluing primitive that descends a sheaf of modules (and a morphism of such
40	sheaves) from per-chart data along an open cover, given a transition cocycle
41	compatible with the cover's own gluing; it is the vehicle that assembles the
42	per-chart free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}^{d}_{U^I}\) and the per-chart quotients
43	\(u^I\) into the global \(\mathcal{U}\) and \(u\), and is set up below.
44	
45	Stating the triple-overlap multiplicativity of the descent datum, however, first
46	requires a small piece of base-change machinery for the pullback functor on sheaves
47	of modules: the three transition isomorphisms attached to a triple of charts live a
48	priori on three different overlaps, and to compare their composite they must be
49	transported to a single common overlap. The next two blocks supply exactly this
50	transport -- the pseudofunctorial comparison of iterated pullbacks
51	(\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}) and its packaging along the triple-overlap data of a
52	scheme glue datum (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) -- after which the
53	cocycle condition can be expressed cleanly.
54	
55	\begin{definition}[Comparison of iterated pullbacks of sheaves of modules]
56	  \label{def:modules_pullbackComp}
57	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp}
58	  \mathlibok
59	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
60	  Let \(a : X \to Y\) and \(b : Y \to Z\) be morphisms of schemes. For a sheaf of
61	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}\), pulling back first along \(b\) and then
62	  along \(a\) agrees with pulling back along the composite \(b \circ a\): there is a
63	  natural isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
64	  \[
65	    a^{*}\bigl(b^{*}\mathcal{M}\bigr)
66	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
67	    (b \circ a)^{*}\mathcal{M},
68	  \]
69	  natural in \(\mathcal{M}\), expressing that \(\mathcal{M} \mapsto \mathcal{M}\)'s
70	  pullback is a pseudofunctor on schemes. These comparison isomorphisms satisfy the
71	  pentagon (associativity) coherence for a triple of composable morphisms. This is the
72	  pullback half of the pseudofunctoriality of sheaves of modules and is supplied by
73	  Mathlib.
74	\end{definition}
75	
76	\begin{lemma}\leanok
77	[Base-change transport of a transition isomorphism to a triple overlap]
78	  \label{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}
79	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackBaseChangeTransport}
80	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp, def:gr_the_glue_data}
81	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) with charts
82	  \(U_i\), overlaps \(V_{ij}\), overlap immersions \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow
83	  U_i\), transitions \(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\), and triple-overlap
84	  data \(t'_{ijk}\) with its compatibility identity \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\). For an index
85	  triple \((i,j,k)\) write
86	  \[
87	    V_{ijk} \;=\; V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}
88	  \]
89	  for the common triple overlap, with the two projections
90	  \(p^{ij}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ij}\) and \(p^{ik}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ik}\), and
91	  let \(t'_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{jki}\) be the glue datum's triple
92	  transition, satisfying
93	  \[
94	    t'_{ijk} \,\circ\, p^{jk}_{jki} \;=\; p^{ij}_{ijk} \,\circ\, t_{ij}
95	    \qquad (t_{\mathrm{fac}}).
96	  \]
97	  Given per-chart sheaves of modules \(\mathcal{M}_i\) and a transition isomorphism
98	  \[
99	    g_{ij} \;:\; f_{ij}^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
100	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
101	    (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j
102	  \]
103	  on the overlap \(V_{ij}\) (as in \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}), the
104	  \emph{base-change transport} of \(g_{ij}\) to the triple overlap \(V_{ijk}\) is the
105	  isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{ijk}}\)-modules
106	  \[
107	    \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}
108	      \;:\;
109	    (f_{ij} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
110	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
111	    (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j
112	  \]
113	  obtained by pulling \(g_{ij}\) back along \(p^{ij}_{ijk}\) and reassociating the
114	  iterated pullbacks via \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. With the analogous transports
115	  \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) (the first taken along
116	  \(t'_{ijk}\), so that \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) identifies its source and target sheaves
117	  with those of \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) and \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\)), all three
118	  transported isomorphisms become endomorphism-composable: \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\)
119	  and \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) land on a common sheaf, and their composite is
120	  comparable to \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) on \(V_{ijk}\).
121	\end{lemma}
122	
123	\begin{proof}
124	  \uses{def:modules_pullbackComp, def:gr_the_glue_data}
125	  The pullback functor on sheaves of modules is a pseudofunctor: for composable
126	  scheme morphisms it carries the comparison isomorphisms of
127	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}, natural in the sheaf and coherent for triple
128	  composites. Pulling the transition isomorphism \(g_{ij}\) back along the projection
129	  \(p^{ij}_{ijk} : V_{ijk} \to V_{ij}\) yields an isomorphism between
130	  \((p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*} f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) and \((p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}(t_{ij} \circ
131	  f_{ji})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\); applying \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} to each side
132	  reassociates these into the single-pullback form
133	  \((f_{ij} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) and
134	  \((t_{ij} \circ f_{ji} \circ p^{ij}_{ijk})^{*}\mathcal{M}_j\), giving
135	  \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\). For the \((j,k)\)-transition the same construction is
136	  applied along the triple transition \(t'_{ijk}\) rather than a bare projection; the
137	  glue datum's compatibility identity \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\),
138	  \(t'_{ijk} \circ p^{jk}_{jki} = p^{ij}_{ijk} \circ t_{ij}\), guarantees that the
139	  source of \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\) coincides with the target of
140	  \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) (both are the pullback of \(\mathcal{M}_j\) along the same
141	  morphism \(V_{ijk} \to U_j\)), so the two are composable as endomorphism-style
142	  isomorphisms over \(V_{ijk}\). The reassociations introduced by
143	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} are coherent (they satisfy the pentagon identity), so
144	  the composite \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i} \circ \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\) and the third
145	  transport \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) are isomorphisms between the same pair of
146	  sheaves on \(V_{ijk}\), and may be compared directly. This is the module-level
147	  analogue of the scheme-level triangle \(t'_{ijk}\)/\(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) and is the
148	  shape in which the multiplicative cocycle condition is stated below.
149	\end{proof}
150	
151	\begin{lemma}[Triple-overlap endpoint bridges]
152	  \label{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
153	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_src,
154	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_mid,
155	        AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueData_bridge_tgt}
156	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data}
157	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) and fix
158	  indices \(i,j,k\) with triple overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}\).
159	  The three transports of \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} have
160	  endpoints pinned down by three scheme-morphism equalities on \(V_{ijk}\), each a
161	  pure consequence of the glue datum's structural identities:
162	  \begin{itemize}
163	    \item (\emph{src}) \(\mathrm{fst} \circ f_{ij} = \mathrm{snd} \circ f_{ik}\)
164	      -- the defining pullback condition of \(V_{ijk}\);
165	    \item (\emph{mid}) \(\mathrm{fst} \circ (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})
166	      = (t'_{ijk} \circ \mathrm{fst}) \circ f_{jk}\) -- the pullback condition
167	      composed with the glue datum's \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) identity;
168	    \item (\emph{tgt}) \((t'_{ijk} \circ \mathrm{fst}) \circ (t_{jk} \circ f_{kj})
169	      = \mathrm{snd} \circ (t_{ik} \circ f_{ki})\) -- the cocycle core, from the
170	      \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) associativity, the reversed pullback condition, the
171	      \(t_{\mathrm{inv}}\) collapse and the glue datum's cocycle identity.
172	  \end{itemize}
173	  These three equalities align the source, middle, and target sheaves of the three
174	  transports so that the cocycle equation (C2) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}
175	  typechecks.
176	\end{lemma}
177	\begin{proof}
178	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data}
179	  Each equality is an identity of morphisms in the category of schemes, proved by
180	  rewriting with the glue datum's axioms: \emph{src} is literally
181	  \(\mathrm{pullback.condition}\); \emph{mid} adds the \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)
182	  factorisation \(D.t_{\mathrm{fac}}\,i\,j\,k\); \emph{tgt} chains
183	  \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)-associativity at \((j,k,i)\), the reversed pullback
184	  condition, an auxiliary collapse combining \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)-associativity at
185	  \((k,i,j)\) with \(t_{\mathrm{inv}}\), and finally the cocycle identity
186	  \(D.\mathrm{cocycle}\,i\,j\,k\). No sheaf-of-modules data enters; these are
187	  bookkeeping equalities of the underlying scheme cover.
188	\end{proof}
189	
190	% HISTORICAL (abandoned route): an earlier draft built the glued sheaf as a hand-rolled
191	% presheaf of compatible families (decls gluePresheaf / gluePresheafModule / gluePresheafIsSheaf).
192	% That route is abandoned; the gluing is now the equalizer of pushforwards described in the
193	% Construction paragraph of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, which subsumes it.
194	
195	\begin{definition}
196	
197	\leanok
198	[Gluing a sheaf of modules along an open cover]
199	  \label{def:scheme_modules_glue}
200	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue}
201	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:modules_pullbackComp,
202	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
203	  Let \(D\) be a glue datum of schemes (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}), with index set \(J\), charts \(U_i\),
204	  pairwise overlaps \(V_{ij}\), open immersions
205	  \(f_{ij} : V_{ij} \hookrightarrow U_i\), and transition isomorphisms
206	  \(t_{ij} : V_{ij} \xrightarrow{\sim} V_{ji}\) satisfying the glue datum's
207	  cocycle conditions; write \(X\) for the glued scheme and
208	  \(\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\) for the canonical open immersions of the
209	  charts. Suppose given:
210	  \begin{itemize}
211	    \item for each \(i \in J\), a sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules \(\mathcal{M}_i\) on the chart \(U_i\);
212	    \item for each pair \((i,j)\), a \emph{transition isomorphism} of sheaves of
213	      modules on the overlap \(V_{ij}\),
214	      \[
215	        g_{ij} \;:\; f_{ij}^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
216	          \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
217	        (t_{ij} \circ f_{ji})^{*}\,\mathcal{M}_j,
218	      \]
219	      comparing the two charts' sheaves after restriction (pullback) to the
220	      common overlap \(V_{ij}\);
221	  \end{itemize}
222	  \emph{subject to the following module cocycle conditions}, compatible with the
223	  scheme-level cocycle of \(D\) (its triple transition data \(t_{ij}\) and the
224	  overlap immersions \(f_{ij}\)), which are required as hypotheses on the family
225	  \((g_{ij})\):
226	  \begin{enumerate}
227	    \item[(C1)] \emph{self-identity:} the self-transition is the identity,
228	      \(g_{ii} = \mathrm{id}\) on \(f_{ii}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) (with
229	      \(V_{ii}\) and \(t_{ii}\) the diagonal data of \(D\));
230	    \item[(C2)] \emph{triple-overlap multiplicativity:} over each common triple
231	      overlap \(V_{ijk} = V_{ij} \times_{U_i} V_{ik}\) the base-change transports
232	      (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the three transition
233	      isomorphisms satisfy
234	      \[
235	        \widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i} \circ \widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}
236	          \;=\; \widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j},
237	      \]
238	      where \(\widehat{g}_{ij}^{\,k}\), \(\widehat{g}_{jk}^{\,i}\),
239	      \(\widehat{g}_{ik}^{\,j}\) are the transports of \(g_{ij}\), \(g_{jk}\),
240	      \(g_{ik}\) to \(V_{ijk}\) through the scheme-level triple transition
241	      \(t'_{ijk}\) and its compatibility \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\), reassociated by the
242	      pullback comparison \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp} so that all three land on
243	      the same sheaf -- the multiplicative, \(\mathrm{GL}_d\)-valued cocycle
244	      condition.
245	  \end{enumerate}
246	  Conditions (C1) and (C2) are exactly the descent datum for the Zariski cover
247	  \(\{\iota_i\}\); it is subject to them that the gluing is well-defined. Out of this
248	  data the construction produces a glued sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
249	  \(\mathcal{M}\) on the glued scheme \(X\).
250	
251	  \emph{Construction.} The category \(\ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of sheaves of
252	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has all limits, and both pushforward along a morphism and
253	  these limits preserve the sheaf condition; the glued sheaf \(\mathcal{M}\) is therefore
254	  built directly as a limit -- an \emph{equalizer of pushforwards} -- rather than through a
255	  hand-built presheaf of compatible families. Concretely, form the two parallel maps of
256	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules
257	  \[
258	    \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i
259	      \;\;\underset{b}{\overset{a}{\rightrightarrows}}\;\;
260	    \prod_{i,j} (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\,\bigl(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i\bigr),
261	  \]
262	  where, on the \((i,j)\)-component, leg \(a\) pushes the \(i\)-th factor forward along the
263	  overlap immersion \(f_{ij}\) using the unit of the pullback--pushforward adjunction
264	  together with the pushforward-composition comparison
265	  \((\iota_i)_{*} \cong (\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}\circ f_{ij}^{*}\) (the dual of
266	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), and leg \(b\) pushes the \(j\)-th factor forward and
267	  additionally transports it across the inverse transition isomorphism \((g_{ij})^{-1}\)
268	  and the glue datum's compatibility identity, so that both legs land on the common overlap
269	  sheaf \((\iota_i \circ f_{ij})_{*}(f_{ij}^{*}\mathcal{M}_i)\). The glued sheaf is the
270	  equalizer
271	  \[
272	    \mathcal{M} \;=\; \operatorname{eq}(a,b) \;\hookrightarrow\; \prod_{i} (\iota_i)_{*}\,\mathcal{M}_i,
273	  \]
274	  the sub-sheaf of tuples of pushed-forward chart sections whose two overlap restrictions
275	  agree -- precisely the compatible families, now realised as a categorical limit rather than
276	  a bespoke presheaf. The cocycle conditions (C1)/(C2) on the family \((g_{ij})\) are
277	  \emph{not} consumed in forming this equalizer object: the limit exists for any family of
278	  transition maps. They are instead what make the equalizer descend correctly, and are
279	  exactly the hypotheses pinned down downstream when the restriction isomorphisms
280	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso} are produced. This specialises effective descent
281	  of sheaves of modules to the concrete open cover \(\{\iota_i : U_i \hookrightarrow X\}\)
282	  supplied by a scheme glue datum, and is the form in which the Grassmannian's
283	  tautological bundle and quotient are built.
284	
285	  The glued sheaf comes with three further structures, each recorded as its own block
286	  downstream: the \emph{restriction isomorphisms} \(\rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}
287	  \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\) compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps
288	  \cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}; the \emph{characterisation up to unique
289	  isomorphism} by the restriction property \cref{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}; and the
290	  \emph{descent of morphisms} -- a family \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
291	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms glues to a unique \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to
292	  \mathcal{N}\) with \(\rho_i^{\mathcal{N}} \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi = \phi_i \circ
293	  \rho_i^{\mathcal{M}}\) \cref{def:gr_modules_glueHom}.
294	\end{definition}
295	
296	\begin{definition}[Restriction isomorphisms of the glued sheaf]
297	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}
298	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueRestrictionIso}
299	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
300	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
301	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
302	  Let \(\mathcal{M}\) be the glued sheaf of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}. For each
303	  chart index \(i\) there is a \emph{restriction isomorphism} of sheaves of
304	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-modules
305	  \[
306	    \rho_i \;:\; \iota_i^{*}\,\mathcal{M} \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathcal{M}_i,
307	  \]
308	  compatible with the transition isomorphisms: over each overlap \(V_{ij}\) the two
309	  restrictions \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\), transported to the common overlap, differ
310	  exactly by \(g_{ij}\).
311	\end{definition}
312	\begin{proof}
313	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso,
314	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
315	  By the equalizer-of-pushforwards description \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue}, a section of
316	  \(\mathcal{M}\) over an open of the form \(\iota_i^{-1}V\), restricted to the chart
317	  \(U_i\), is recovered from and recovers its \(i\)-th component
318	  \(s_i \in \Gamma(\mathcal{M}_i, \cdot)\) -- the \(i\)-th factor of the product
319	  \(\prod_i (\iota_i)_{*}\mathcal{M}_i\) into which the equalizer embeds; the
320	  chart-restriction bridge \cref{lem:over_restrict_pullback_iso} identifies the pullback
321	  \(\iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}\) with this restriction, and the \(i\)-th projection
322	  \((s_\bullet) \mapsto s_i\) is the isomorphism \(\rho_i\) (with inverse extending a chart
323	  section by the overlap relation).
324	  The overlap compatibility is the defining relation of the compatible families: over
325	  \(V_{ij}\) the components \(s_i, s_j\) are related by \(g_{ij}\), which is precisely the
326	  statement that \(\rho_i\) and \(\rho_j\) differ by \(g_{ij}\) after transport to
327	  \(V_{ij}\); the triple-overlap coherence needed for this to be consistent is (C2),
328	  whose transports are aligned by \cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport} and
329	  \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}.
330	\end{proof}
331	
332	\begin{lemma}[Uniqueness of the glued sheaf up to canonical isomorphism]
333	  \label{lem:gr_modules_glue_unique}
334	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glue_unique}
335	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
336	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
337	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
338	  Suppose \(\mathcal{M}'\) is another sheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules equipped with
339	  isomorphisms \(\rho'_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}' \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}_i\)
340	  compatible with the \(g_{ij}\) over the overlaps. Then there is a unique isomorphism
341	  \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) intertwining the restriction
342	  isomorphisms, i.e.\ with \(\rho'_i \circ \iota_i^{*}(\cdot) = \rho_i\) for every \(i\).
343	\end{lemma}
344	\begin{proof}
345	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
346	  A morphism of sheaves of modules on \(X\) is determined by, and may be assembled from,
347	  its restrictions to the members of the open cover \(\{\iota_i\}\), agreeing on the
348	  overlaps -- the locality and gluing of sections
349	  (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom. The
350	  intertwining condition forces the restriction over \(U_i\) to be
351	  \((\rho'_i)^{-1} \circ \rho_i : \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M}'\);
352	  these local isomorphisms agree on each overlap \(V_{ij}\) because both
353	  \((\rho_i)\) and \((\rho'_i)\) are compatible with the \emph{same} transition family
354	  \((g_{ij})\) (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}). By locality they glue to a
355	  unique global isomorphism \(\mathcal{M} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{M}'\) with the
356	  required property; uniqueness is the separation half of the same gluing.
357	\end{proof}
358	
359	\begin{definition}[Descent of morphisms along the cover]
360	  \label{def:gr_modules_glueHom}
361	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.glueHom}
362	  % NOTE: forward declaration (planned work); the \lean{} target is not yet realised.
363	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_glue, def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso,
364	    lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
365	  Let \((\mathcal{M}_i, g_{ij})\) and \((\mathcal{N}_i, h_{ij})\) be two glued data
366	  over the same glue datum \(D\), with glued sheaves \(\mathcal{M}, \mathcal{N}\) and
367	  restriction isomorphisms \(\rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i, \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i\). Given a family
368	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\)-linear morphisms \(\phi_i : \mathcal{M}_i \to \mathcal{N}_i\)
369	  commuting with the transition isomorphisms on every overlap (i.e.\ \(h_{ij}\) intertwines
370	  the pullbacks of \(\phi_i\) and \(\phi_j\) over \(V_{ij}\)), there is a unique morphism
371	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) with
372	  \[
373	    \rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i \circ \iota_i^{*}\phi \;=\; \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i
374	    \qquad \text{for every } i.
375	  \]
376	\end{definition}
377	\begin{proof}
378	  \uses{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso, lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}
379	  On each chart the prescribed identity defines a local morphism
380	  \((\rho^{\mathcal{N}}_i)^{-1} \circ \phi_i \circ \rho^{\mathcal{M}}_i :
381	  \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{M} \to \iota_i^{*}\mathcal{N}\). The commutation of the \(\phi_i\)
382	  with the transition isomorphisms \((g_{ij}, h_{ij})\), together with the overlap
383	  compatibility of the \(\rho\)-families (\cref{def:gr_modules_glueRestrictionIso}), makes
384	  these local morphisms agree on every overlap \(V_{ij}\). By the locality and gluing of
385	  sections (\cref{lem:sheaf_existsUnique_gluing_mathlib}) applied to the internal hom they
386	  promote to a unique global morphism \(\phi : \mathcal{M} \to \mathcal{N}\) restricting
387	  to each \(\phi_i\) through the \(\rho_i\) -- the descent of \(\mathrm{Hom}\) along the
388	  cover.
389	\end{proof}

391	\subsection{Pullback of free sheaves along an arbitrary morphism}
392	\label{sec:grquot_pullback_free}
393	
394	The functor of points (\cref{def:grassmannian_functor}) acts on morphisms by pullback, so
395	it needs the pullback of the trivial bundle to be again trivial --
396	\(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I} \cong \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}\) -- for an
397	\emph{arbitrary} scheme morphism \(\varphi\). Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison applies
398	only when the underlying site functor \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is \emph{final}; the
399	following three blocks remove that restriction for every morphism, and the resulting
400	pullback machinery preserves rank-\(d\) local freeness.
401	
402	\begin{lemma}\leanok
403	[The opens-pullback functor is final]
404	  \label{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
405	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.opensMap_final}
406	  For any morphism of schemes \(\varphi : T' \to T\), the inverse-image functor on opens
407	  \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi : \mathrm{Opens}(T) \to \mathrm{Opens}(T')\),
408	  \(U \mapsto \varphi^{-1}U\), is \emph{final}.
409	\end{lemma}
410	
411	\begin{proof}
412	  Finality means that for every open \(V \subseteq T'\) the structured-arrow category of
413	  opens \(U \subseteq T\) with \(V \le \varphi^{-1}U\) is connected. That category is
414	  nonempty and has the terminal object \(U = \top\) -- one always has
415	  \(V \le \varphi^{-1}\top = T'\), and every object admits a unique arrow to \(\top\) --
416	  and a category with a terminal object is connected.
417	\end{proof}
418	
419	\begin{lemma}\leanok
420	[Pullback of a free sheaf is free]
421	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
422	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso}
423	  \uses{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
424	  For any scheme morphism \(\varphi : T' \to T\) and index type \(I\), there is a canonical
425	  isomorphism of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_{T'}\)-modules
426	  \[
427	    \varphi^{*}\bigl(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I}\bigr)
428	      \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
429	    \mathcal{O}_{T'}^{\,\oplus I}.
430	  \]
431	\end{lemma}
432	
433	\begin{proof}
434	  \uses{lem:gr_opensMap_final}
435	  Mathlib's pullback-of-free comparison produces this isomorphism whenever the site functor
436	  \(\mathrm{Opens.map}\,\varphi\) is final; that finality is \cref{lem:gr_opensMap_final},
437	  which holds for every morphism, so the comparison applies unconditionally.
438	\end{proof}
439	
440	\begin{lemma}
441	
442	\leanok
443	[Free-pullback comparison is natural in the base morphism]
444	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom}
445	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_eqToHom}
446	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
447	  Let \(\varphi, \psi : T' \to T\) be morphisms with \(\varphi = \psi\) and \(I\) an index
448	  type. The two free-pullback comparisons \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} along \(\varphi\) and
449	  \(\psi\) agree after transporting the differing pullback sources by the canonical
450	  \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\): the composite of that transport with
451	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\psi)\) equals \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi)\).
452	\end{lemma}
453	\begin{proof}
454	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
455	  Substituting the equality \(\varphi = \psi\) collapses the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) to the
456	  identity and the two comparisons coincide.
457	\end{proof}
458	
459	\begin{lemma}
460	
461	\leanok
462	[Iso-level free-pullback cancellation]
463	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso}
464	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_trans_symm_eqToIso}
465	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
466	  For equal base morphisms \(\varphi = \psi : T' \to T\) and index type \(I\), the composite
467	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi) \circ \mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\psi)^{-1}\) is the
468	  \(\mathrm{eqToIso}\) transport between the (differing) pullback sources of the free sheaf
469	  \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,\oplus I}\). Stated and proved generically in the base morphisms, so its
470	  use never forces evaluation of a concrete immersion; this is the load-bearing replacement
471	  for the cast chain in the self-identity \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_id}.
472	\end{lemma}
473	\begin{proof}
474	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
475	  Substituting \(\varphi = \psi\) turns the composite into
476	  \(\mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi) \circ \mathrm{pullbackFreeIso}(\varphi)^{-1} =
477	  \mathrm{id}\), which is the \(\mathrm{eqToIso}\) of the now-trivial source equality.
478	\end{proof}
479	
480	\begin{lemma}\leanok
481	[Pullback preserves rank-\(d\) local freeness]
482	  \label{lem:gr_pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
483	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank}
484	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:modules_pullbackComp}
485	  Let \(\varphi : T' \to T\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{M}\) a sheaf of
486	  \(\mathcal{O}_T\)-modules locally free of rank \(d\)
487	  (\cref{def:is_locally_free_of_rank}). Then the pullback \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M}\) is
488	  locally free of rank \(d\) on \(T'\).
489	\end{lemma}
490	
491	\begin{proof}
492	  \uses{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:is_locally_free_of_rank, def:modules_pullbackComp}
493	  Choose an open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(T\) trivialising \(\mathcal{M}\),
494	  \(\mathcal{M}|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\,d}\). The preimages
495	  \(\{\varphi^{-1}U_i\}\) cover \(T'\). On each member the factorisation
496	  \(\varphi \circ \iota_{\varphi^{-1}U_i} = (\varphi|_{U_i}) \circ \iota_{U_i}\) of the
497	  restricted morphism, together with the pseudofunctor comparison of iterated pullbacks
498	  (\cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}), identifies
499	  \((\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M})|_{\varphi^{-1}U_i}\) with
500	  \((\varphi|_{U_i})^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U_i}^{\,d}\); the latter is
501	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\varphi^{-1}U_i}^{\,d}\) by \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}. Hence
502	  \(\varphi^{*}\mathcal{M}\) is locally free of rank \(d\).
503	\end{proof}
504	
505	\section{The tautological quotient on the charts}
506	\label{sec:grquot_chart}
507	
508	\emph{Infrastructure: scalar endomorphisms.} Realising a matrix of regular
509	functions as a morphism of free sheaves of modules has no off-the-shelf primitive
510	in Mathlib; it is assembled from two small project-local helpers that turn a global
511	function into a scalar endomorphism of the structure sheaf, after which the matrix
512	is reconstituted entry-by-entry over a finite biproduct.
513	
514	\begin{definition}\leanok
515	[Global section of the unit module]
516	  \label{def:gr_globalUnitSection}
517	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.globalUnitSection}
518	  \uses{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
519	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and \(a \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) a global section of
520	  the structure sheaf. Viewing \(\mathcal{O}_X\) as the unit module
521	  \(\mathbf{1} \in \ShMod(\mathcal{O}_X)\) (\cref{lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}), the
522	  section \(a\) determines a global section of \(\mathbf{1}\): on each open
523	  \(V \subseteq X\) take the restriction \(a|_V \in \Gamma(V, \mathcal{O}_X)\), the
524	  family \((a|_V)_V\) being compatible with the restriction maps because
525	  restriction is functorial. This is the section of the unit sheaf attached to a
526	  global function.
527	\end{definition}
528	
529	\begin{definition}\leanok
530	[Scalar endomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)]
531	  \label{def:gr_scalarEnd}
532	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd}
533	  \uses{def:gr_globalUnitSection, lem:moduleUnit_mathlib}
534	  For \(a \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the \emph{scalar endomorphism}
535	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) : \mathcal{O}_X \to \mathcal{O}_X\) is the
536	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-linear endomorphism of the unit module given by multiplication
537	  by \(a\). It is obtained from the global section of \cref{def:gr_globalUnitSection}
538	  under the canonical identification of endomorphisms of the unit module
539	  \(\mathbf{1}\) with its global sections, \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong
540	  \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\); concretely, on each open \(V\) it acts as
541	  multiplication by \(a|_V\). These scalar endomorphisms are the matrix-entry
542	  building blocks of the chart quotient.
543	\end{definition}
544	
545	\begin{lemma}\leanok
546	[Scalar endomorphism of the unit is the identity]
547	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}
548	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_one}
549	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
550	  The scalar endomorphism attached to the unit function \(1 \in
551	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) is the identity of the unit module:
552	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}\).
553	\end{lemma}
554	
555	\begin{proof}
556	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
557	  Multiplication by \(1\) is the identity on every section, so under the
558	  identification \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) the section \(1\)
559	  corresponds to \(\mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}\).
560	\end{proof}
561	
562	\begin{lemma}\leanok
563	[Scalar endomorphism of the zero function is zero]
564	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
565	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_zero}
566	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
567	  The scalar endomorphism attached to the zero function \(0 \in
568	  \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) is the zero endomorphism:
569	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\).
570	\end{lemma}
571	
572	\begin{proof}
573	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
574	  Multiplication by \(0\) sends every section to \(0\), so the corresponding
575	  endomorphism of the unit module is the zero map.
576	\end{proof}
577	
578	\begin{lemma}\leanok
579	[Composition of scalar endomorphisms is multiplication]
580	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}
581	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_comp}
582	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
583	  For \(a, b \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) the scalar endomorphisms compose by
584	  multiplication: \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) \circ \mathrm{scalarEnd}(b) =
585	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a\,b)\).
586	\end{lemma}
587	\begin{proof}
588	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
589	  Under the identification \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) the
590	  scalar endomorphisms act as multiplication by \(a\) and by \(b\); their composite
591	  is multiplication by \(a\,b\), because the section ring is commutative and
592	  restriction maps are ring homomorphisms.
593	\end{proof}
594	
595	\begin{lemma}\leanok
596	[Scalar endomorphism is additive]
597	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
598	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_add}
599	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
600	  For \(a, b \in \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\) one has \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a + b) =
601	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}(a) + \mathrm{scalarEnd}(b)\).
602	\end{lemma}
603	\begin{proof}
604	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
605	  Multiplication by \(a + b\) equals multiplication by \(a\) plus multiplication by
606	  \(b\) on every section, since the restriction maps of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) are additive;
607	  under \(\End(\mathbf{1}) \cong \Gamma(X, \mathbf{1})\) this is the stated additivity.
608	\end{proof}
609	
610	\begin{lemma}
611	
612	\leanok
613	[Scalar endomorphism of a finite sum]
614	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_sum}
615	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_sum}
616	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
617	  For a finite index set \(s\) and a family \(f : \iota \to \Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)\),
618	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(\sum_{i \in s} f_i\bigr) = \sum_{i \in s} \mathrm{scalarEnd}(f_i)\).
619	\end{lemma}
620	\begin{proof}
621	  \uses{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
622	  Induction on the finite set \(s\): the empty case is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
623	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}) and the insertion step is additivity
624	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}).
625	\end{proof}
626	
627	\emph{Infrastructure: matrix automorphisms of the free sheaf.} To realise the
628	\(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transitions one must promote an invertible \(d \times d\)
629	matrix of global functions to an \emph{automorphism} of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf
630	\(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\), exactly as the chart quotient \cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}
631	realises the universal matrix. The next blocks assemble this matrix-to-morphism
632	functor and record its two structural identities -- that it carries matrix
633	multiplication to composition and the identity matrix to the identity -- which are
634	the algebraic heart of the bundle cocycle.
635	
636	\begin{definition}\leanok
637	[Matrix endomorphism of the free sheaf]
638	  \label{def:gr_matrixEnd}
639	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd}
640	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd}
641	  Let \(S\) be a scheme, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
642	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) a matrix of
643	  global functions. The \emph{matrix endomorphism} \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the
644	  endomorphism of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) whose
645	  \((p,q)\)-component, under the presentation of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) as the
646	  finite biproduct of \(d\) copies of the unit module \(\mathbf{1}\), is the scalar
647	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{p,q})\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}). It is
648	  assembled from these components by the universal property of the rank-\(d\)
649	  biproduct, exactly as the chart quotient \(u^I\) is assembled
650	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}). This is the substitute for the (non-existent)
651	  Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) endomorphism of a free sheaf'' primitive.
652	\end{definition}
653	
654	\begin{lemma}\leanok
655	[Matrix endomorphism carries multiplication to composition]
656	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
657	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_comp}
658	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
659	  For \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) the matrix
660	  endomorphisms compose according to the matrix product, with the order reversed by
661	  the contravariance of the component indexing:
662	  \[
663	    \mathrm{matrixEnd}(M) \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)
664	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEnd}(N \cdot M).
665	  \]
666	\end{lemma}
667	\begin{proof}
668	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp, lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}
669	  The composition of two biproduct-assembled morphisms is again biproduct-assembled,
670	  with \((i,q)\)-component the sum over the middle index \(p\) of the composites of
671	  components, \(\sum_p \mathrm{component}_{i,p} \circ \mathrm{component}_{p,q}\) (the
672	  categorical matrix product over the biproduct). Each composite of scalar
673	  endomorphisms is the scalar endomorphism of the product,
674	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\circ\mathrm{scalarEnd}(b) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(ab)\)
675	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_comp}), and a finite sum of scalar endomorphisms is the
676	  scalar endomorphism of the sum (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_add}); hence the \((i,q)\)-component
677	  of the composite is \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(\sum_p M_{q,p} N_{p,i}\bigr) =
678	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl((N\cdot M)_{q,i}\bigr)\), which is precisely the
679	  \((i,q)\)-component of \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N\cdot M)\). The reversal of the factor
680	  order is the contravariance of the column/component indexing.
681	\end{proof}
682	
683	\begin{lemma}\leanok
684	[Matrix endomorphism of the identity is the identity]
685	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}
686	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_one}
687	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
688	  The matrix endomorphism of the identity matrix is the identity morphism of
689	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\): \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(1_{d\times d}) =
690	  \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}}\).
691	\end{lemma}
692	\begin{proof}
693	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
694	  The diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\)
695	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the off-diagonal components are
696	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the morphism with this
697	  component matrix is the identity of the biproduct.
698	\end{proof}
699	
700	\begin{definition}\leanok
701	[Free-sheaf automorphism of an invertible matrix]
702	  \label{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}
703	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso}
704	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp, lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}
705	  Let \(M, N \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d \times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\) be mutually
706	  inverse, \(M N = 1\) and \(N M = 1\). The \emph{matrix automorphism}
707	  \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(M, N)\) is the isomorphism of free sheaves
708	  \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) with forward map
709	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) and inverse map \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(N)\); the two
710	  composites are identities by \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp} (turning the products
711	  \(NM\) and \(MN\) into the relevant compositions) and \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_one}.
712	  Its forward map is \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) by construction
713	  (\cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom}).
714	\end{definition}
715	
716	\begin{lemma}\leanok
717	[Forward map of the matrix automorphism]
718	  \label{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom}
719	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso_hom}
720	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEnd}
721	  The forward map of \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(M, N)\) is \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\).
722	\end{lemma}
723	\begin{proof}
724	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, def:gr_matrixEnd}
725	  Immediate from the definition \cref{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso}, whose forward field is
726	  literally \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\).
727	\end{proof}
728	
729	\begin{lemma}\leanok
730	[Matrix automorphisms compose multiplicatively]
731	  \label{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul}
732	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixToFreeIso_mul}
733	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
734	  Let \(A, A', B, B'\) be \(d \times d\) matrices of global functions on \(S\) with
735	  \(A A' = A' A = 1\), \(B B' = B' B = 1\), so that \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(A, A')\)
736	  and \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(B, B')\) are automorphisms of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\).
737	  Their forward maps compose to the forward map of the product automorphism, with the
738	  order reversed by the component contravariance:
739	  \[
740	    \mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(A, A')_{\mathrm{hom}}
741	      \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}(B, B')_{\mathrm{hom}}
742	      \;=\; \mathrm{matrixEnd}(B \cdot A).
743	  \]
744	  Equivalently, composition of matrix automorphisms realises matrix multiplication of
745	  the underlying matrices. This is the linkage that turns the matrix-level cocycle
746	  identity into a composition identity of sheaf-of-module isomorphisms.
747	\end{lemma}
748	\begin{proof}
749	  \uses{def:gr_matrixToFreeIso, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom, lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}
750	  By \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_hom} the two forward maps are
751	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(A)\) and \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(B)\); their composite is
752	  \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(A)\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(B) = \mathrm{matrixEnd}(B\cdot A)\) by
753	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_comp}.
754	\end{proof}
755	
756	\begin{definition}
757	
758	\leanok
759	[Chart quotient \(u^I\)]
760	  \label{def:gr_chart_quotient}
761	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap}
762	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd}
763	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", \S 1,
764	  % sub-section "Universal quotient" (read from
765	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L898-L910).
766	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
767	  % "We next define a rank $d$ locally free sheaf $\U$ on $\grass(r,d)$
768	  %  together with a surjective homomorphism $\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{\grass(r,d)} \to \U$.
769	  %  On each $U^I$ we define a surjective homomorphism
770	  %  $u^I :\oplus^r\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I} \to  \oplus^d\,{\mathcal O}_{U^I}$
771	  %  by the matrix $X^I$."
772	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
773	  Let \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) with \(\#(I) = d\). On the affine chart
774	  \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) define the
775	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}\)-linear homomorphism of trivial bundles
776	  \[
777	    u^I \;:\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}
778	      \;\longrightarrow\; \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}
779	  \]
780	  given by left multiplication by the universal \(d \times r\) matrix \(X^I\)
781	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}): on global sections it is the \(R^I\)-linear
782	  map \((R^I)^{r} \to (R^I)^{d}\), \(v \mapsto X^I v\). Since the \(I\)-th minor
783	  \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\) is the identity matrix \(1_{d \times d}\), the composite of
784	  \(u^I\) with the inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates
785	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \hookrightarrow \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) is the
786	  identity; hence \(u^I\) is a split surjection of locally free sheaves, with
787	  target the free rank-\(d\) sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\).
788	
789	  \emph{Realisation.} The free sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) and
790	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\) are the finite biproducts (coproducts) of copies of
791	  the unit module \(\mathbf{1} = \mathcal{O}_{U^I}\), indexed by \(\{1,\dots,r\}\)
792	  and \(\{1,\dots,d\}\). Each entry \(X^I_{p,q} \in R^I\) of the universal matrix
793	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}) is a global function on \(U^I = \Spec R^I\),
794	  obtained from \(R^I\) through the canonical identification
795	  \(\Gamma(\Spec R^I, \mathcal{O}) \cong R^I\); the corresponding scalar
796	  endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,q}) : \mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}\)
797	  (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is the \((p,q)\)-component of \(u^I\). Assembling these
798	  components over the index biproducts -- the universal property of the biproduct
799	  packaging a matrix of component morphisms into a single morphism of the free
800	  sheaves -- yields \(u^I\). This entry-by-entry assembly via the finite biproduct
801	  is the substitute for a (non-existent) Mathlib ``matrix \(\mapsto\) morphism of
802	  free sheaves'' primitive.
803	\end{definition}
804	
805	\begin{lemma}\leanok
806	[The $I$-columns of the chart quotient are the identity]
807	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
808	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_ιFree}
809	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
810	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
811	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
812	  For each \(k \in \{1,\dots,d\}\), the chart quotient \(u^I\)
813	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) carries the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th basis section of the
814	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\) -- where \(\iota_I(k)\) is the \(k\)-th
815	  element of \(I\) in increasing order -- to the \(k\)-th basis section of
816	  \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\); that is, the \(I\)-indexed columns of \(u^I\) form the
817	  identity:
818	  \[
819	    e_{\iota_I(k)} \,\circ\, u^I \;=\; e_k.
820	  \]
821	\end{lemma}
822	
823	\begin{proof}
824	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_scalarEnd,
825	    lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero,
826	    lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
827	  By the biproduct presentation of \(u^I\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}), the
828	  composite \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I\) reads off the \(\iota_I(k)\)-th column of the
829	  component matrix, whose \(p\)-th entry is the scalar endomorphism
830	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)})\). The \(I\)-indexed columns of the
831	  universal matrix \(X^I\), read through the order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong
832	  I\), form the \(d \times d\) identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}),
833	  so \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\). Hence the diagonal entry is
834	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
835	  off-diagonal entries are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\)
836	  (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}); the resulting column is the \(k\)-th coordinate
837	  inclusion, giving \(e_{\iota_I(k)} \circ u^I = e_k\).
838	\end{proof}

840	\begin{lemma}\leanok
841	[The chart quotient is an epimorphism]
842	  \label{lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_epi}
843	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartQuotientMap_epi}
844	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient}
845	  The chart quotient \(u^I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\)
846	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_quotient}) is an epimorphism of sheaves of modules.
847	\end{lemma}
848	
849	\begin{proof}
850	  \uses{def:gr_chart_quotient, lem:gr_chartQuotientMap_iFree}
851	  We exhibit a section, so that \(u^I\) is a \emph{split} epimorphism and hence an
852	  epimorphism. Let \(\iota_I : \{1,\dots,d\} \hookrightarrow \{1,\dots,r\}\) be the
853	  inclusion of the \(I\)-indexed coordinates, \(k \mapsto \iota_I(k)\), the
854	  \(k\)-th element of \(I\) in increasing order. It induces a morphism of free
855	  sheaves \(s_I : \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \to \mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,r}\), the
856	  coordinate inclusion sending the \(k\)-th basis section to the
857	  \(\iota_I(k)\)-th. We claim
858	  \[
859	    u^I \circ s_I \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}}.
860	  \]
861	  Both sides are morphisms out of the free rank-\(d\) sheaf, which is the coproduct
862	  of \(d\) copies of the unit module; by the universal property of that coproduct
863	  it suffices to check the equality on each coordinate, i.e.\ to compare component
864	  matrices. The \((p,k)\)-component of \(u^I \circ s_I\) is the entry
865	  \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)}\) of the universal matrix at the \(I\)-indexed column, which
866	  is precisely the \(I\)-th minor \(X^I_I\) of \(X^I\). Since that minor is the
867	  \(d \times d\) identity (the columns of \(X^I\) indexed by \(I\), read through the
868	  order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \cong I\), form the identity block --
869	  \cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}), one has \(X^I_{p,\iota_I(k)} = \delta_{p,k}\),
870	  so \(u^I \circ s_I\) has the identity component matrix and equals
871	  \(\mathrm{id}\). Hence \(s_I\) splits \(u^I\) and \(u^I\) is a split
872	  epimorphism, in particular an epimorphism.
873	\end{proof}

875	\section{The \(\mathrm{GL}_d\) bundle transition cocycle}
876	\label{sec:grquot_bundle_cocycle}
877	
878	The universal quotient sheaf \(\mathcal{U}\) (\cref{def:gr_universal_quotient_sheaf}) is
879	obtained by feeding the gluing primitive \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} the per-chart free
880	rank-\(d\) sheaves \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d}\), the bundle transitions
881	\(g_{I,J} = (X^I_J)^{-1}\), and the two module cocycle hypotheses (C1)/(C2) that primitive
882	requires. The matrix cocycle for the Cramer inverses \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) is already in hand --
883	it is the content of the scheme-level cocycle \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} and the minor identities
884	\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities} -- but the gluing primitive consumes its
885	\emph{module-level} incarnation: an honest isomorphism of sheaves of modules together with
886	its self-identity and triple-overlap multiplicativity. The three blocks of this section
887	package exactly that, decoupling the construction of \(\mathcal{U}\) from the remaining
888	matrix-to-module transport bookkeeping. This is the only net-new infrastructure the
889	universal quotient requires beyond the gluing primitive itself.

902	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}

904	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), and \(1^{-1} = 1\).

1081	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
1082	
1083	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
1084	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
1085	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
1086	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
1087	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
1088	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
1089	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
1090	
1091	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
1092	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
1093	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
1094	unchanged. -/
1095	
1096	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
1097	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
1098	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
1099	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
1100	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
1101	  F : T.Modules
1102	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
1103	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
1104	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
1105	  epi : Epi q
1106	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
1107	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
1108	
1109	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
1110	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
1111	coincide). -/
1112	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
1113	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
1114	
1115	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
1116	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
1117	
1118	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
1119	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
1120	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
1121	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
1122	
1123	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
1124	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
1125	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
1126	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)
1127	  exact ⟨f ≪≫ g,
1128	    (congrArg (x.q ≫ ·) (Iso.trans_hom f g)).trans <|
1129	      (Category.assoc x.q f.hom g.hom).symm.trans <|
1130	        (congrArg (· ≫ g.hom) hf).trans hg⟩
1131	
1132	/-- The equivalence-of-quotients setoid on `RankQuotient r d T`. -/
1133	instance rqSetoid (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) : Setoid (RankQuotient r d T) where
1134	  r := RankQuotient.Rel
1135	  iseqv := ⟨RankQuotient.rel_refl, RankQuotient.rel_symm, RankQuotient.rel_trans⟩
1136	
1137	/-- The **pullback action** on a rank-`d` quotient: pull the target sheaf and quotient map
1138	back along `ψ`, re-presenting the source as the trivial bundle via `pullbackFreeIso`. The
1139	result is again an epimorphism (pullback preserves epis) onto a rank-`d` locally free sheaf
1140	(`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank`). -/
1141	noncomputable def rqPullback {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
1142	    (x : RankQuotient r d T) : RankQuotient r d T' where
1143	  F := (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).obj x.F
1144	  q := (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q
1145	  epi :=
1146	    -- fully explicit: the def-diamond on `T.Modules` blocks `Epi`-instance search, so
1147	    -- `x.epi` is threaded through `map_epi`/`epi_comp` by hand
1148	    @CategoryTheory.epi_comp _ _ _ _ _
1149	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv inferInstance
1150	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q)
1151	      (@CategoryTheory.Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ) inferInstance _ _
1152	        x.q x.epi)
1153	  locFree := Scheme.Modules.pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank ψ x.locFree
1154	
1155	/-- The pullback action respects the equivalence relation, hence descends to quotients. -/
1156	lemma rqPullback_rel {r d : ℕ} {T' T : Scheme.{0}} (ψ : T' ⟶ T)
1157	    {x y : RankQuotient r d T} (h : x.Rel y) :
1158	    (rqPullback ψ x).Rel (rqPullback ψ y) := by
1159	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
1160	  refine ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).mapIso f, ?_⟩
1161	  change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map x.q) ≫
1162	      (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map f.hom
1163	    = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map y.q
1164	  rw [Category.assoc, ← (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map_comp]
1165	  exact congrArg
1166	    (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso ψ (Fin r)).inv ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback ψ).map m) hf
1167	
1168	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

1483	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(r,d)` (`def:grassmannian_functor`): the
1484	contravariant functor from schemes to sets sending `T` to the set of equivalence classes
1485	of rank-`d` locally free quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F`, acting on morphisms by pullback.
1486	
1487	The object and morphism assignments are complete; the functoriality laws (`map_id`,
1488	`map_comp`) are discharged — via the naturality of the pseudofunctor comparison isomorphisms
1489	`pullbackId`/`pullbackComp` of `Scheme.Modules.pullback` — through the free-sheaf coherences
1490	`pullbackFreeIso_id`/`pullbackFreeIso_comp`, which reduce by coproduct extensionality to the
1491	unit-level coherences `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_id`/`pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`. Fully proved. -/
1492	noncomputable def functor (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.{0}ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type 1 where
1493	  obj T := Quotient (rqSetoid r d T.unop)
1494	  map {X Y} g := TypeCat.ofHom (Quotient.map (rqPullback (r := r) (d := d) g.unop)
1495	    (fun _ _ h => rqPullback_rel g.unop h))
1496	  map_id X := by
1497	    -- reduce to the equivalence relation on representatives
1498	    ext z
1499	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
1500	    | _ x =>
1501	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (𝟙 X).unop x) = Quotient.mk _ x
1502	      -- the canonical iso `(𝟙)^* x.F ≅ x.F` is `pullbackId`; the quotient-map equation it
1503	      -- must satisfy reduces, by naturality of `pullbackId.hom`, to the free coherence
1504	      -- `pullbackFreeIso (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app (free _)`, hence to the unit-level identity
1505	      -- `pullbackObjUnitToUnit (𝟙) = (pullbackId).app unit`. That coherence between
1506	      -- `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjFreeIso` and Mathlib's `pullbackId` is the open obstacle.
1507	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨(Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).app x.F, ?_⟩
1508	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (𝟙) x).q` and `(pullbackId.app x.F).hom` (defeq)
1509	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (𝟙 X.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1510	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X.unop)).map x.q) ≫
1511	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.app x.F = x.q
1512	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X.unop).hom.naturality x.q,
1513	        ← Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_id]
1514	      -- `(𝟭).map x.q = x.q` is only defeq, so close by term (rw can't see through it)
1515	      exact Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc _ _
1516	  map_comp {X Y Z} f g := by
1517	    ext z
1518	    induction z using Quotient.ind with
1519	    | _ x =>
1520	      change Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback (f ≫ g).unop x)
1521	        = Quotient.mk _ (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x))
1522	      -- the canonical iso `(g.unop ≫ f.unop)^* x.F ≅ g.unop^*(f.unop^* x.F)` is `pullbackComp`;
1523	      -- the quotient-map equation reduces, by naturality, to the composite free coherence
1524	      -- relating `pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop)` to `pullbackFreeIso g.unop`/`f.unop`
1525	      -- through `pullbackComp` — the composite analogue of the `map_id` obstacle.
1526	      refine Quotient.sound ⟨((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).app x.F).symm, ?_⟩
1527	      -- unfold `(rqPullback (g∘f) x).q` and `(pullbackComp.app x.F).symm.hom` (defeq), writing
1528	      -- the composite as `g.unop ≫ f.unop` so the `pullbackComp` naturality matches syntactically
1529	      change ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1530	          (Scheme.Modules.pullback (g.unop ≫ f.unop)).map x.q) ≫
1531	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app x.F
1532	        = (rqPullback g.unop (rqPullback f.unop x)).q
1533	      -- expose the `pullbackComp.inv` naturality square (mirrors the `map_id` reduction)
1534	      rw [Category.assoc, (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.naturality x.q]
1535	      -- the composite free coherence (`pullbackFreeIso_comp`) in inverse form: invert both
1536	      -- sides of the iso equation `pullbackComp.hom.app free ≫ pfba.hom = (pullback g).map pfa.hom
1537	      -- ≫ pfb.hom`.
1538	      have hstar : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).inv ≫
1539	            (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).inv.app (SheafOfModules.free (Fin r))
1540	          = (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso g.unop (Fin r)).inv ≫
1541	            (Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map
1542	              (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv := by
1543	        have hH := Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso_comp f.unop g.unop (Fin r)
1544	        rw [← cancel_epi ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).hom.app
1545	          (SheafOfModules.free (Fin r)) ≫
1546	          (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso (g.unop ≫ f.unop) (Fin r)).hom)]
1547	        trans (𝟙 _)
1548	        · rw [Category.assoc, Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
1549	          exact (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g.unop f.unop).hom_inv_id_app _
1550	        · rw [hH]; simp
1551	      -- whisker `hstar` by `≫ (pullback f ⋙ pullback g).map x.q` and refold the RHS via
1552	      -- `map_comp` into `(rqPullback g (rqPullback f x)).q`.
1553	      exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans
1554	        ((congrArg (· ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pullback f.unop ⋙
1555	              Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map x.q) hstar).trans
1556	          ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans
1557	            (congrArg ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso g.unop (Fin r)).inv ≫ ·)
1558	              ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.unop).map_comp
1559	                (Scheme.Modules.pullbackFreeIso f.unop (Fin r)).inv
1560	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback f.unop).map x.q)).symm)))
1561	
1562	/-- **`Gr(d,r)` represents the Grassmannian functor** (`thm:grassmannian_universal_property`):
1563	the tautological quotient `⟨U, u⟩` exhibits `Gr(d,r)` as the fine moduli space of rank-`d`
1564	quotients of `O^r`, i.e. `Hom(T, Gr(d,r)) ≅ Grass(r,d)(T)` naturally in `T`.
1565	
1566	NOTE: `functor` and `Scheme.Modules.glue` have landed; this rides on `tautologicalQuotient`
1567	(hence on the bundle cocycle, the only remaining upstream gap). Body = the local-to-global
1568	inverse construction of Nitsure §1 (a `T`-point of `Gr` is a rank-`d` quotient; conversely a
1569	quotient determines, chart by chart, a map to each `U^I`, glued via the universal property of
1570	the glued scheme). -/
1571	noncomputable def represents (d r : ℕ) (hd : 1 ≤ d) (hdr : d ≤ r) :
1572	    (functor d r).RepresentableBy (scheme d r) :=
1573	  sorry
1574	
1575	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

951	  condition (C1) of \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} for the bundle data \((g_{I,J})\).
952	\end{lemma}
953	\begin{proof}
954	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
955	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_scalarEnd_one, lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}
956	  The \(I\)-th minor of the universal matrix is the identity block, \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\)
957	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}), so its Cramer inverse is again the
958	  identity, \((X^I_I)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}).
959	  Hence in the biproduct presentation of \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition} the diagonal
960	  components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(1) = \mathrm{id}\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_one}) and the
961	  off-diagonal components are \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(0) = 0\) (\cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_zero}), so
962	  the assembled automorphism is the identity of \(\mathcal{O}_{U^I_I}^{\,d}\); conjugation by
963	  the free-pullback comparisons preserves the identity, giving \(g_{I,I} = \mathrm{id}\).
964	\end{proof}

966	\begin{definition}
967	
968	\leanok
969	[Bundle transition data over the glue datum]
970	  \label{def:gr_bundleTransitionData}
971	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransitionData}
972	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, def:scheme_modules_glue}
973	  The family of bundle transitions \((g_{I,J})\) (\cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}), repackaged
974	  over the index set of the Grassmannian glue datum \(\mathrm{theGlueData}(d,r)\) in the exact
975	  shape \(\bigl(\forall I\,J,\; f_{IJ}^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^I}^{\,d} \cong (t_{IJ} \circ
976	  f_{JI})^{*}\mathcal{O}_{U^J}^{\,d}\bigr)\) that the gluing primitive
977	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_glue} consumes.

980	\begin{lemma}\leanok
981	[Cramer-inverse cocycle on the triple overlap]
982	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}
983	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix}
984	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
985	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
986	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
987	  Over the common triple-overlap ring (the localisation carrying the three charts'
988	  data to \(V_{IJK}\)) the Cramer inverses of the localised minors satisfy the
989	  multiplicative cocycle identity
990	  \[
991	    (X^J_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}.
992	  \]
993	  This is the pure matrix-algebra core of (C2), independent of any sheaf data.
994	\end{lemma}
995	\begin{proof}
996	  \uses{def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
997	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
998	    lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel, lem:gr_cocycle}
999	  Write all matrices over the common triple-overlap ring. The image matrix of the
1000	  transition \(\theta_{I,J}\) is \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), and its \(K\)-minor is
1001	  computed by restricting to the \(K\)-indexed columns; since taking a column
1002	  submatrix commutes with left multiplication (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}),
1003	  \[
1004	    X^J_K \;=\; \bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_K .
1005	  \]
1006	  Both \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\) have unit determinant
1007	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}), so this product of invertibles inverts
1008	  by \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\):
1009	  \[
1010	    (X^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1}\,(X^I_J).
1011	  \]
1012	  Multiplying on the right by \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) and cancelling \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1\)
1013	  via the two-sided Cramer identity (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}, the
1014	  cancellation packaged in \cref{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}) gives
1015	  \((X^J_K)^{-1} (X^I_J)^{-1} = (X^I_K)^{-1}\). The agreement of the three image
1016	  matrices over the triple overlap that licenses these manipulations is the content of
1017	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}, the matrix form of the scheme-level cocycle
1018	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}.
1019	\end{proof}

1021	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1022	[Scalar endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1023	  \label{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}
1024	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scalarEnd_pullback}
1025	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso, def:modules_pullbackComp}
1026	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(a \in \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S)\) a
1027	  global function, with comorphism on global sections
1028	  \(p^{\sharp} : \Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S) \to \Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T)\).
1029	  Write \(q : p^{*}\mathbf{1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathbf{1}\) for the unit-pullback comparison
1030	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at a one-element index). Then the pullback of the scalar endomorphism
1031	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) is conjugate, through \(q\), to the scalar endomorphism of the
1032	  base-changed function:
1033	  \[
1034	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\bigr)
1035	      \;=\; q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{scalarEnd}\bigl(p^{\sharp} a\bigr) \,\circ\, q
1036	  \]
1037	  as endomorphisms of \(p^{*}\mathbf{1}\); equivalently
1038	  \(q \circ p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)) = \mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp} a) \circ q\). This is the
1039	  single-entry naturality atom underlying the matrix-level statement
1040	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}.
1041	\end{lemma}
1042	\begin{proof}
1043	  \uses{def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1044	  The scalar endomorphism \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a)\) (\cref{def:gr_scalarEnd}) is multiplication
1045	  by the global section \(a\) on the unit module \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}_S\). Pulling back
1046	  along \(p\) sends multiplication by \(a\) to multiplication by its image, and the comorphism
1047	  \(p^{\sharp}\) is precisely the action of pullback on global functions, so
1048	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(a))\) is multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\). The
1049	  comparison \(q\) is an isomorphism of unit modules intertwining this multiplication on
1050	  \(p^{*}\mathbf 1\) with multiplication by \(p^{\sharp}a\) on \(\mathbf 1\); conjugating back
1051	  along \(q\) therefore yields the claimed identity. The verification is a single computation on
1052	  the unit module, where multiplication by a function commutes with the natural comparison
1053	  \(q\).
1054	\end{proof}
1055	
1056	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1057	[Matrix endomorphism is natural under pullback]
1058	  \label{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback}
1059	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.matrixEnd_pullback}
1060	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, def:gr_scalarEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1061	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1062	  Let \(p : T \to S\) be a morphism of schemes, \(d \in \mathbb{N}\), and
1063	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{O}_S))\). Let
1064	  \(p^{\sharp}M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(\Gamma(T, \mathcal{O}_T))\) be the entrywise
1065	  image of \(M\) under the comorphism \(p^{\sharp}\), and let
1066	  \(Q : p^{*}(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}) \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) be the free-pullback
1067	  comparison \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} at the rank-\(d\) index set. Then the
1068	  pullback of the matrix endomorphism \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is conjugate, through \(Q\), to
1069	  the matrix endomorphism of the base-changed matrix:
1070	  \[
1071	    p^{*}\bigl(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\bigr)
1072	      \;=\; Q^{-1} \,\circ\, \mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M) \,\circ\, Q .
1073	  \]
1074	\end{lemma}
1075	\begin{proof}
1076	  \uses{def:gr_matrixEnd, lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso,
1077	    def:modules_pullbackComp}
1078	  By \cref{def:gr_matrixEnd}, \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)\) is the biproduct-assembled endomorphism
1079	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d} = \bigoplus_{k} \mathbf 1\) whose \((k,\ell)\)-component is
1080	  \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(M_{k,\ell})\). The pullback functor \(p^{*}\) is a left adjoint, hence
1081	  additive and biproduct-preserving; through the coproduct comparison underlying the
1082	  free-pullback isomorphism \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso} it carries the biproduct presentation
1083	  of \(\mathcal{O}_S^{\,d}\) to that of \(\mathcal{O}_T^{\,d}\) and the assembled morphism to the
1084	  morphism reassembled from the pulled-back components, the reassociation of the iterated
1085	  pullbacks being \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}. On each single \((k,\ell)\)-component the
1086	  pulled-back scalar endomorphism is, by \cref{lem:gr_scalarEnd_pullback}, the
1087	  comparison-conjugate of \(\mathrm{scalarEnd}(p^{\sharp}M_{k,\ell}) =
1088	  \mathrm{scalarEnd}((p^{\sharp}M)_{k,\ell})\). Reassembling over the biproduct, the conjugating
1089	  one-element comparisons combine into the single free comparison \(Q\), giving
1090	  \(p^{*}(\mathrm{matrixEnd}(M)) = Q^{-1}\circ\mathrm{matrixEnd}(p^{\sharp}M)\circ Q\).
1091	\end{proof}

1093	\begin{lemma}[Base-change bridge to the localised cocycle ring homomorphisms]
1094	  \label{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge}
1095	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.baseChange_bridge}
1096	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_the_glue_data,
1097	    lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_incl_left,
1098	    def:gr_away_incl_right}
1099	  Fix indices \(I, J, K\) and the triple overlap \(V_{IJK}\) of the chart cover
1100	  \(\{U^I\}\) of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}, \cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}).
1101	  The scheme-pullback base-change map on global sections
1102	  \(\Gamma(U^I_J, \mathcal{O}) \to \Gamma(V_{IJK}, \mathcal{O})\) induced by the first projection
1103	  \(p^{IJ}_{IJK}\), and likewise the maps induced by the second projection and by the triple
1104	  transition \(t'_{IJK}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}), are identified -- through the natural identification of global sections of an affine scheme
1105	  with its coordinate ring and the fact that the projections and triple transition are the
1106	  \(\Spec\) morphisms of the coordinate-ring homomorphisms by which the Grassmannian glue datum
1107	  (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) is assembled -- with the ring homomorphisms
1108	  \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left}),
1109	  \(\mathrm{awayInclRight}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_right}) and \(\Theta_{IJ}\)
1110	  (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}) over which the matrix cocycle
1111	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} is stated. Consequently the entrywise image of a Cramer
1112	  inverse \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) under the geometric base-change map agrees with its image under the
1113	  corresponding coordinate-ring homomorphism, so \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} applies
1114	  to the base-changed matrices.
1115	\end{lemma}
1116	\begin{proof}
1117	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij,
1118	    def:gr_away_incl_left, def:gr_away_incl_right}
1119	  The charts \(U^I\) and their overlaps are affine, identified with the spectra of the localised
1120	  coordinate rings; under this identification the global-sections comorphism of a morphism of
1121	  affine charts is the ring homomorphism that induces it on spectra. The triple overlap
1122	  \(V_{IJK}\) is the spectrum of the triple-overlap localisation, and the two projections and
1123	  the triple transition \(t'_{IJK}\) of the glue datum (\cref{def:gr_transition},
1124	  \cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) are, on charts, the spectra of \(\mathrm{awayInclLeft}\),
1125	  \(\mathrm{awayInclRight}\) and \(\Theta_{IJ}\) respectively -- the precise form in which the
1126	  chart transitions and glue datum are assembled from the transition maps. Applying the
1127	  global-sections identification to each morphism translates its geometric base-change map on
1128	  global sections into the named ring homomorphism, whence the entrywise images of the Cramer
1129	  inverses coincide.
1130	\end{proof}

1132	\begin{lemma}[Transport and endpoint alignment of the bundle transitions]
1133	  \label{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_transport}
1134	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.bundleTransition_cocycle_transport}
1135	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1136	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback, lem:gr_baseChange_bridge,
1137	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
1138	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1139	  Fix indices \(I, J, K\) with triple overlap \(V_{IJK} = U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\),
1140	  and let \(\widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}, \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I}, \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\) be
1141	  the base-change transports (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) of the
1142	  three bundle transitions to \(V_{IJK}\). Under the free-pullback comparisons
1143	  (\cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}) and the endpoint bridges
1144	  (\cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}), each transported transition is identified with the
1145	  matrix automorphism of the corresponding base-changed Cramer inverse on the common
1146	  free sheaf \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\); granting the matrix identity
1147	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}, the composite satisfies
1148	  \[
1149	    \widehat{g}_{JK}^{\,I} \circ \widehat{g}_{IJ}^{\,K}
1150	      \;=\; \widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}
1151	  \]
1152	  as isomorphisms of sheaves of modules on \(V_{IJK}\). This is the substantive
1153	  transport step: the alignment of the three transports' domains and codomains.
1154	\end{lemma}
1155	\begin{proof}
1156	  \uses{def:gr_bundleTransition, lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix, lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul,
1157	    lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback, lem:gr_baseChange_bridge,
1158	    lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport, lem:gr_glueData_bridges,
1159	    def:modules_pullbackComp, lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}
1160	  This is pure assembly of the two infrastructure pieces over the endpoint bridges. Each
1161	  bundle transition is, by \cref{def:gr_bundleTransition}, \(\mathrm{matrixToFreeIso}\) of a
1162	  localised Cramer inverse conjugated by the free-pullback comparisons
1163	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackFreeIso}; its base-change transport
1164	  (\cref{lem:modules_pullback_basechange_transport}) reassociates the iterated pullbacks via
1165	  \cref{def:modules_pullbackComp}, and the three endpoint bridges \cref{lem:gr_glueData_bridges}
1166	  pin the source, middle and target sheaves to a single \(\mathcal{O}_{V_{IJK}}^{\,d}\). By
1167	  \cref{lem:gr_matrixEnd_pullback} each transport then equals \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\) of the
1168	  base-changed Cramer inverse, and by \cref{lem:gr_baseChange_bridge} those base-changed matrices
1169	  are exactly \((X^I_J)^{-1}, (X^J_K)^{-1}, (X^I_K)^{-1}\) over the common triple-overlap ring of
1170	  \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix}. The linkage \cref{lem:gr_matrixToFreeIso_mul} composes the
1171	  first two to \(\mathrm{matrixEnd}\bigl((X^J_K)^{-1}(X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\), and
1172	  by the matrix cocycle identity \cref{lem:gr_bundleCocycle_matrix} the argument equals
1173	  \((X^I_K)^{-1}\), so the composite equals \(\widehat{g}_{IK}^{\,J}\); the bridges make both
1174	  sides isomorphisms between the same pair of sheaves on \(V_{IJK}\).
1175	\end{proof}
